details: The “Atithi Uttarakhand Home-Stay Registration” scheme has been introduced by the Department of Tourism, Government of Uttarakhand. It aims to promote rural tourism and generate local employment by enabling residents to register their homes as certified home-stays that offer safe, affordable accommodation and meal facilities to tourists in Uttarakhand.
benefits: Under this scheme, any house owner in the state who wishes to make the residential rooms of their house available to tourists can register their home with the Tourism Department as a home-stay. After registration, the owner can provide night stay and meals to guests and charge a fee for these services. The main objective of this scheme is to offer accommodation and food facilities to tourists and to provide employment opportunities to rural residents at their homes.

After registration, the respective accommodation is listed on the department’s website under the Home Stay directory, allowing any tourist to obtain information from the website and avail night stay facilities.
eligibility: For registration under this scheme, a No Objection Certificate (NOC) must be issued by the Development Authority/Local Body in urban areas, and by the Gram Pradhan in rural areas.
The accommodation unit must be entirely residential, and the house owner must reside there with their family.
There must be at least one and a maximum of six rooms available for guests/tourists.
The residential unit must have a toilet, should be clean and well-maintained, equipped with fire safety equipment, and built in a structurally robust manner.

online: Registration for Atithi Uttarakhand Home-Stay has to be done online at

> Trade > Homestay Registration, for which Aadhaar number and the Aadhaar-linked mobile number are mandatory, and during the registration process, the applicant must attach the affidavit mentioned in the application form, PAN card, a copy of the approved building map (plan), photographs of the home-stay (including the home-stay name, room interiors, toilet, and kitchen), a copy of land ownership documents (Khata, Khatauni/Registry papers), the old registration certificate of the Paying Guest House (in case of an old unit), the registration fee - ₹500 through NEFT/online/offline, the character certificate issued by the district administration, and the Fire Department NOC/fire extinguisher bill (as specified by the District Tourism Development Officer).

Thereafter, an inspection is carried out by departmental officers and the registration number is provided to the applicant; Once registered, the owner can offer night stay facilities to guests/tourists by charging the prescribed fee.

documents_required: Affidavit as mentioned in the application form
Photographs of the home stay (including name board, room interiors, toilet, and kitchen)
Land ownership documents (Khata, Khatauni / Registry papers)
Previous Paying Guest House registration certificate (if applicable)
Character certificate issued by the District Administration
Fire Department NOC / Fire Extinguisher bill (as prescribed by the District Tourism Development Officer)

faqs.question: What is the main objective of this scheme?
faqs.answer: The main objective is to provide accommodation and food facilities to tourists while creating employment opportunities for rural residents within their own homes.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to register under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Any house owner in Uttarakhand who is willing to offer residential rooms to tourists and resides in the same house with their family is eligible.

faqs.question: How many rooms can be registered for home stay purposes?
faqs.answer: A minimum of one and a maximum of six rooms can be registered.

faqs.question: Is it mandatory for the homeowner to live in the same house?
faqs.answer: Yes, the home stay must be part of a residential unit where the owner and their family live.

faqs.question: What are the basic requirements for the accommodation?
faqs.answer: The accommodation must be clean, well-maintained, equipped with fire safety tools, structurally sound, and must have a toilet.

faqs.question: How can one apply for home stay registration?
faqs.answer: Applications must be submitted online at https://homestay.uttarakhandtourism.gov.in/ .

faqs.question: What is the registration fee?
faqs.answer: The registration fee is ₹500/-, payable via NEFT, online, or offline.

faqs.question: Who conducts the inspection after submission?
faqs.answer: Departmental officers of the Tourism Department conduct an on-site inspection.

faqs.question: When is the registration number issued?
faqs.answer: The registration number is issued after verification and successful inspection by officials.
